mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal broad economic strength and expanding aggregate demand, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery shows consistent job growth, supporting the equity bull market.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts, falling more than 10% over 3 months, signals a significant tightening of financial conditions or a collapse in consumer confidence, presaging a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market collapse was a key leading indicator for the 2008 Global Financial Crisis.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) signal stagflationary pressures. This compresses corporate profit margins and increases the risk of a hawkish monetary policy response, driving negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-mo avg YoY CPIAUCSL > 3% AND 3-mo avg YoY INDPRO < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged bear market for equities.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal does not remain statistically significant (p < 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already low (e.g., below 5%), further declines signal an overheating labor market. This increases wage-push inflation fears and the probability of pre-emptive monetary tightening by the Fed, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: doc:Standard Phillips Curve theory relating low unemployment to inflation.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE < 5.0%, the correlation between changes in UNRATE and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which is a primary driver of corporate revenue and earnings growth, thus supporting positive equity market returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in retail sales and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant positive acceleration in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, boosting near-term earnings expectations and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of The Conference Board's Coincident Economic Index.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of positive 5-day returns) following a trigger event is not statistically different from the unconditional baseline hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is at the Zero Lower Bound (ZLB), an increase in inflation lowers real yields and signals that monetary policy is gaining traction against deflation. This boosts risk assets as deflationary fears recede.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has held the policy rate near zero since late 2008.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between monthly changes in CPIAUCSL and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of real GDP is the broadest measure of economic health. A higher growth rate implies a stronger environment for corporate earnings, supporting higher equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity prices are a discounted stream of future earnings, which are fundamentally tied to GDP growth.
failure_condition: The correlation between YoY GDPC1 growth and subsequent quarterly S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a full business cycle.
